message:
fix(telemetry/protocol): disambiguate piggybacked telemetry marker

extract_piggybacked_telemetry() used `rfind(PIGGYBACK_MARKER)` and
unconditionally treated everything after the match as the telemetry
JSON trailer. A compiler/runner error message that happened to print
the literal marker string mid-line would cause the build output to
be silently truncated at the match site.

Apply two disambiguations before accepting the match:

1. The marker must start a line (position 0 or preceded by '\n').
   A mid-line occurrence is almost certainly user log text.
2. The content after the marker must begin with '{' after trimming.
   If it doesn't look like JSON, treat the marker as coincidental
   and return the full original output untouched.

Only when both checks pass do we consume the trailer. If serde_json
still rejects the body, keep the existing behavior — preserve
pre-marker text as build_output and surface extraction_error — since
the worker clearly meant to send telemetry but emitted something
malformed.

Regression test added for the mid-line marker case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
